hints.to eat: 'to eat' is 'edere' — 'es' here is from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to go out: 'to go out' is 'exire' — not related to 'es'

hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sis' — 'es' is indicative
hints.Perfect indicative: Perfect of 'esse' would be 'fuisti' — 'es' is present
hints.Imperfect indicative: Imperfect would be 'eras' — 'es' is present indicative

hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat) — 'es' here is from 'esse' (to be)
hints.eo, ire, ivi, itum: These are the parts of 'ire' (to go) — not related
hints.possum, posse, potui: These are the parts of 'posse' (to be able) — a compound of 'esse' but different

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'Dominus' — 'Domine' with -e ending is vocative of 2nd declension
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'Domino' — 'Domine' is the vocative form of direct address

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'Dominus, Domini' is 2nd declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension vocative matches nominative — 'Domine' with -e ending is 2nd declension vocative
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us/-u with different genitive — 'Dominus, Domini' is 2nd declension

hints.Praising: 'Praising' would be 'laudans' (present participle) — 'laudabilis' is a passive-sense adjective
hints.Laudatory: 'Laudatory' implies giving praise — 'laudabilis' means deserving of praise
hints.Praised: 'Praised' would be 'laudatus' (past participle) — 'laudabilis' means 'able to be praised / praiseworthy'

hints.Weakly: 'Weakly' would be 'infirme' — 'valde' means the opposite: 'greatly / strongly'

hints.Psalm 23: 'Dominus regit me': Psalm 23 is about the Lord as shepherd — Augustine here echoes Psalm 145's praise formula
hints.Psalm 51: 'Miserere mei Deus': Psalm 51 is a penitential psalm — Augustine echoes the doxological Psalm 145
hints.Psalm 110: 'Dixit Dominus Domino meo': Psalm 110 is messianic — Augustine echoes the praise of Psalm 145

translation: Great are you, O Lord, and exceedingly worthy of praise.
